items.title: WTI holds above $100 as Trump threats to obliterate Iran's energy infrastructure boost Crude Oil
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Crude Oil held above the $100 per barrel mark on Monday, trading in a wide intraday range that saw prices spike toward session highs near $101 before a sharp, short-lived dip back below $98.

items.title: CEE FX: Inflation and energy shock pressure currencies – ING
items.summary: ING’s Frantisek Taborsky expects a risk-off tone in CEE markets as higher Oil prices and geopolitical tensions test already elevated central bank hike pricing.

items.title: NZD/USD falls sharply, hitting its lowest level since November near 0.5700
items.summary: NZD/USD trades around 0.5715 on Monday, down 0.60% on the day at the time of writing, after falling earlier to its lowest level since November near 0.5700. The pair remains under heavy pressure as the US Dollar (USD) strengthens amid rising geopolitical tensions and a cautious market environment.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average rebounds as Trump hints at Iran discussions
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) trades higher on Monday, adding back around 415 points in a thin recovery from last week's late plunge as President Donald Trump suggested a resolution to the war with Iran may be within reach. The S&P 500 rose 0.5% and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.3%.

items.title: Euro area: Inflation jump and hawkish ECB stance – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale economists expect Euro area headline inflation to rise sharply in March, driven by higher energy costs, while core inflation eases slightly.

items.title: Powell speech: Policy in good place to wait and see
items.summary: Federal Reserve (Fed) Chairman Jerome Powell said on Monday that there is tension between the Fed's two objectives, during a moderated discussion at the Harvard University Principles of Economics Class in Cambridge, per Reuters.

items.title: BoC: Dovish tone and long hold signalled – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities analysts anticipate the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) March Summary of Deliberations will maintain a dovish tone, stressing excess supply and disinflation.

items.title: Germany: War-driven energy shock risks ECB hike – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Ralph Solveen notes that Germany’s inflation rate rose from 1.9% to 2.7% in March 2026 as energy prices surged due to the war in Iran, while core inflation stayed at 2.5%.

items.title: GBP: Repricing risks into spring – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that the Pound has been the second best performing G10 currency after the Dollar since the Middle East conflict, driven by a sharp repricing of Bank of England (BoE) policy expectations.

items.title: Eurozone: Inflation fears rise on war shock – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chief Economist Bert Colijn highlights that Eurozone sentiment weakened in March as the Middle East war hit confidence. The economic sentiment indicator dropped from 98.3 in February to 96.6, with businesses and consumers more worried about inflation.

items.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent: Oil market is well supplied
items.summary: In an interview with Fox News on Monday, United States (US) Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the Oil market is well supplied but added that any additional supply will be helpful, per Reuters.

items.title: NOK: Energy support and Norges Bank hawkish shift – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG analysts note that NOK has outperformed most European peers during the Middle East conflict, supported by Norway’s energy exporter status and a hawkish repricing of Norges Bank policy.
